results.report_text:
Big Bank night-sweep report
Timestamp basis: latest available market/filing data through 2026-06-01.

Scope assumption
- Interpreted “Big Bank” as the major U.S. money-center / large-bank group:
  JPM, BAC, WFC, C, GS, MS.

Executive summary
- Broad read is constructive: 5 of 6 names have recent EPS beats; 4 of 6 also beat revenue estimates.
- Best fundamental momentum in the latest quarter: MS, C, and GS.
- Best scale/quality balance: JPM remains the strongest all-around franchise.
- Cheapest on a bank-style valuation basis: C and BAC; WFC also screens inexpensive.
- Most expensive: MS and GS, justified by stronger capital-markets / wealth franchises and recent execution.
- Tape was mixed-to-positive: C, GS, and MS were up on the day; JPM, BAC, and WFC were slightly lower.
- News flow was mostly neutral and heavily dominated by filings/aggregation rather than high-conviction editorial headlines.

Market snapshot
Ticker | Price | Day chg | Mkt cap | P/E | P/B | ROE | Latest read
---|---:|---|---|---|---|---|---
JPM | 296.74 | -0.86% | $800.3B | 14.47x | 2.21x | 15.7% | Still the cleanest all-around large bank
BAC | 51.50 | -0.19% | $365.5B | 12.10x | 1.22x | 10.5% | Cheap-ish, decent beat, improving momentum
WFC | 77.36 | -0.23% | $236.7B | 11.36x | 1.33x | 11.9% | Inexpensive, but Q1 revenue miss/margin pressure
C | 128.97 | +2.44% | $220.1B | 13.01x | 1.04x | 8.35% | Lowest P/B; strong Q1 beat and guidance
GS | 1048.23 | +2.21% | $309.2B | 17.75x | 2.52x | 14.8% | Strong quarter, premium valuation
MS | 210.98 | +1.43% | $332.8B | 18.44x | 2.91x | 15.8% | Best recent growth profile, richest valuation

Latest quarter highlights
- JPM
  - Q1 2026 revenue: $49.8B, +10.0% YoY
  - Q1 2026 EPS: $5.94, +17.2% YoY
  - Operating margin expanded 521 bps YoY
  - TTM revenue: $182.4B; TTM net income: $57.0B
  - Latest 10-Q accession: `0001628280-26-029344`
  - Read: very solid, broad-based franchise strength; not the cheapest, but still the benchmark name in the group.

- BAC
  - Q1 2026 revenue: $30.27B, +10.6% YoY, beat estimate by 1.3%
  - Q1 2026 EPS: $1.11, beat estimate by 11.0%
  - Net income: $8.58B, +16.1% YoY
  - TTM revenue: $112.2B; TTM net income: $31.6B
  - Latest 10-Q accession: `0000070858-26-000249`
  - Read: better-than-expected quarter; valuation remains reasonable, but it still trades like a second-tier large bank versus JPM.

- WFC
  - Q1 2026 revenue: $21.45B, missed estimate by 1.1%
  - Q1 2026 EPS: $1.60, beat estimate by 1.3%
  - Revenue +6.4% YoY; EPS +15.1% YoY
  - Operating margin contracted 364 bps YoY
  - TTM revenue: $83.9B; TTM net income: $21.2B
  - Latest 10-Q accession: `0000072971-26-000217`
  - Read: still inexpensive, but the quarter was mixed and the operating-momentum signal is less clean than peers.

- C
  - Q1 2026 revenue: $24.63B, beat estimate by 4.0%
  - Q1 2026 EPS: $3.06, beat estimate by 15.9%
  - Net income +42.4% YoY; revenue +14.1% YoY
  - Management reiterated FY2026 ROTCE guidance of 10%–11%
  - TTM revenue: $90.0B; TTM net income: $17.6B
  - Latest 10-Q accession: `0000831001-26-000019`
  - Read: strongest “catch-up” story in the pack if execution holds; very low P/B supports the thesis.

- GS
  - Q1 2026 revenue: $17.23B, beat estimate by 1.7%
  - Q1 2026 EPS: $17.55, beat estimate by 7.6%
  - Net income +18.8% YoY; revenue +14.4% YoY
  - Q2 2026 dividend guidance initiated at $4.50/share
  - TTM revenue: $62.1B; TTM net income: $18.2B
  - Latest 10-Q accession: `0000886982-26-000118`
  - Read: premium franchise, strong quarter, but valuation already reflects quality.

- MS
  - Q1 2026 revenue: $20.58B, beat estimate by 3.9%
  - Q1 2026 EPS: $3.43, beat estimate by 12.5%
  - Net income +30.7% YoY; revenue +16.0% YoY
  - TTM revenue: $73.3B; TTM net income: $18.0B
  - Latest 10-Q accession: `0000895421-26-000121`
  - Read: best near-term growth among the group; valuation is the richest, but momentum is real.

News and filing read-through
- JPM
  - Recent headlines were mostly financing-related:
    - callable notes due 2046
    - $500M 2030 notes issuance
  - Interpretation: neutral-to-positive; signals active capital-markets funding access, but not a fundamental surprise.
  - Recent 8-K activity exists, but nothing screamed distress.

- BAC
  - Recent headlines were dominated by:
    - personnel / Merrill-related story
    - institutional ownership updates
  - Interpretation: mostly noisy/automated; little fundamental signal from the headlines.
  - Earnings/filing data is the more useful input here.

- GS
  - Recent headlines mostly concern institutional ownership changes.
  - Interpretation: low editorial quality, but no obvious negative flag.
  - The dividend guidance in the 8-K/earnings release is the meaningful positive signal.

- WFC / C / MS
  - No useful company-news items were returned in the latest fetch.
  - Interpretation: absence of indexed news is not itself meaningful; rely more on the earnings/SEC read.

Reliability note
- The news feed is heavily skewed toward aggregation, holdings, and other automated posts.
- I weighted SEC filings and earnings releases more heavily than headline tone.
- For banks, valuation ratios like gross margin, current ratio, and debt ratios can be misleading; P/B, ROE, EPS trend, revenue trend, and capital-return signals are more useful.

Overall assessment
- Best quality: JPM.
- Best current momentum: MS and C.
- Best value: C and BAC, with WFC also cheap but less clean on the latest quarter.
- Best risk/reward if execution continues: C.
- Highest-quality premium names: JPM, GS, MS.
- Weakest tape / mixedest quarter: WFC.

Logging-ready conclusion
- Big Bank complex remains fundamentally constructive.
- Q1 2026 results were broadly strong, with beats concentrated in BAC, C, GS, and MS.
- JPM continues to anchor the group on scale and stability.
- C looks most interesting on valuation vs. improving fundamentals.
- WFC is the main “watch for proof” name due to revenue miss and margin contraction.
- News flow does not currently show a broad negative catalyst across the group.

If you want, I can also convert this into a stricter log format with fields like:
`timestamp | universe | catalyst | bullish / neutral / bearish | evidence | action`.

main_watch_items: Interest-rate path / net interest income sensitivity
universe_summary.universe_takeaway: Big Bank complex remains fundamentally constructive; Q1 2026 results were broadly strong, with beats concentrated in BAC, C, GS, and MS. JPM anchors on scale/stability; C looks most interesting on valuation vs. improving fundamentals; WFC is the main watch-for-proof name due to revenue miss and margin contraction.
